lane_0_pressure.reason: Internal tool-call gate passed; plan MCP credential/runtime repair without starting external MCP.

servers.name: tavily
servers.notes: Key embedded in URL query param; 405 error risk from description note

servers.name: exa
servers.notes: EXA_API_KEY embedded in config; stdio process spawn

servers.name: lark
servers.notes: Placeholders $LARK_APP_ID/$LARK_APP_SECRET in args; stdio process spawn

tavily.config_status: key in URL, env also present

lane_4_tavily_config_debt.endpoint_risk_source: extensions_config.json description field explicitly states 'MCP endpoint returns 405'
lane_4_tavily_config_debt.recommended_fix: Remove key from URL; use ${TAVILY_API_KEY} env var in Authorization header via SSE headers; investigate 405 root cause
lane_4_tavily_config_debt.config_migration_path: Change url to 'https://mcp.tavily.com/mcp' and set Authorization header with Bearer token from ${TAVILY_API_KEY}
lane_4_tavily_config_debt.risk_if_not_fixed: Key exposure in logs/URL; 405 errors blocking Tavily MCP at runtime

exa_gap.failure_mode: MultiServerMCPClient stdio spawn fails; npx exa-mcp exits with auth error

lark_gap.notes: Lark credentials present in backend/.env; placeholders in config match env var pattern

lane_6_runtime_init_risk.langchain_mcp_adapters_behavior: MultiServerMCPClient.get_tools() calls all servers; single failure may block all tools if not isolated
failure_modes: tavily: SSE connection failure or 405 error blocks tavily tools; 405 documented in config description
exa: stdio npx spawn fails if EXA_API_KEY is missing/invalid; exits immediately
lark: stdio pnpm spawn with env vars from .env; should succeed if pnpm and @larksuiteoapi/lark-mcp installed
recommended_runtime_probe_order: 1. lark (credentials present, stdio; easiest to validate)
2. tavily (key present, but 405 risk must be investigated first)
lane_6_runtime_init_risk.isolated_probe_note: MultiServerMCPClient does not support per-server isolation in get_tools(); a single failing server blocks the entire call

lane_7_repair_strategy_options.name: Disable missing-credential servers
lane_7_repair_strategy_options.notes: Disables exa; preserves tavily/lark; but doesn't fix tavily URL-embedded key or 405 risk

lane_7_repair_strategy_options.name: User provides missing credentials
lane_7_repair_strategy_options.notes: Waits for EXA_API_KEY; doesn't resolve tavily 405

lane_7_repair_strategy_options.name: Tavily-only first: fix URL + investigate 405
lane_7_repair_strategy_options.notes: Fixes tavily key-in-URL but 405 documented in config; needs runtime probe to confirm

lane_7_repair_strategy_options.name: MCP all-disabled baseline → then per-server enable
lane_7_repair_strategy_options.notes: Set all 3 enabled servers to enabled=false; verify agent works without MCP; then enable lark first (has credentials), probe health; then tavily; then exa

lane_7_repair_strategy_options.name: Minimal config sanitize + disable exa (composite)
lane_7_repair_strategy_options.notes: Disable exa (no credentials) + sanitize tavily URL (remove embedded key); leave lark enabled; this is a practical subset of D

phase_sequence: R147A: Config sanitize (tavily URL key removal, exa disable) + smoke test
R147B: Lark runtime probe (credentials present, isolated stdio)
R147C: Tavily runtime probe (URL fixed, 405 investigation)
R147D: Exa enable (after user provides EXA_API_KEY)
lane_8_r147_authorization_package.runtime_probe_restriction: MCP server health probe only; no external tool calls; no real LLM tool invocation
validation_plan: 2. Remove tavilyApiKey from tavily URL in extensions_config.json
4. Create smoke harness: create_deerflow_agent with empty tools list; verify no MCP init
5. Enable only lark; run single lark health probe (no tool calls)
6. Verify no 405 on tavily after URL fix
7. If probe passes, enable tavily; if 405 persists, investigate
lane_8_r147_authorization_package.rollback_plan: Restore extensions_config.json from git; R146 snapshot preserved in migration_reports

lane_9_mcp_readiness_matrix.blocker: key embedded in URL; 405 error documented in config description
lane_9_mcp_readiness_matrix.next_action: R147A: Sanitize URL, remove embedded key, investigate 405 before runtime probe

lane_9_mcp_readiness_matrix.blocker: EXA_API_KEY missing from environment; hardcoded in config
lane_9_mcp_readiness_matrix.next_action: R147A: Disable exa; R147D: User provides EXA_API_KEY then re-enable

lane_9_mcp_readiness_matrix.blocker: None — has credentials, stdio transport; should be probed first
lane_9_mcp_readiness_matrix.next_action: R147B: Lark runtime health probe (stdio, no tool calls)

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: Tavily MCP server URL contains API key as query parameter (?tavilyApiKey=tvly-dev-...). Key also exists in TAVILY_API_KEY env. Key should be removed from URL.
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147A authorized to fix: remove key from URL, use Authorization header or env var

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: extensions_config.json description for tavily explicitly states 'MCP endpoint returns 405'. This is a known failure mode documented by previous developers.
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147A must investigate 405 root cause: wrong endpoint path, auth method mismatch, or server-side issue

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: EXA_API_KEY is MISSING from environment variables. The config has a hardcoded value (6d0efd65-***). Server is enabled but will fail at runtime.
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147A: Disable exa server; R147D: User provides EXA_API_KEY, then re-enable

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: LARK_APP_ID and LARK_APP_SECRET are both PRESENT in environment. Lark server has placeholders $LARK_APP_ID/$LARK_APP_SECRET in args that resolve via env.
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: Lark is ready for runtime probe in R147B

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: MultiServerMCPClient.get_tools() does not support per-server isolation. A single failing server (tavily 405, exa auth) blocks the entire tools loading.
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: Use sequential enable + probe strategy: disable all → enable lark → probe → enable tavily → probe → enable exa

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: Recommended probe order: lark (credentials + stdio) → tavily (URL fixed + 405 resolved) → exa (credentials provided)
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147B probes lark; R147A fixes tavily first; R147D handles exa last

lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.description: extensions_config.json tavily server has key embedded in URL. R147A authorized to remove key from URL and sanitize config.
lane_10_unknown_registry_updates.fix_applied: R147A: Remove ?tavilyApiKey=... from tavily URL; disable exa; preserve lark enabled
